pages.raw_text:
Approaching the intense
interest in the outcome of
the Haywood trial The
Press was prepared Satur-
day night and Sunday
morning to flash the verdict
on the street in an extra
edition as soon as 1t ar
rived.


In consequence The Press
had the news on the street
half an hour in advance of
the Spokesman.Review, al
though that paper had just
finished printing its morn
ing edition, it's men on hand
and machinery warm and in
shape for fast work.


This clean beat was
achieved in spite of the
boosted service of the AS
ISOLATED Press, which had
been, or should have been,
in close touch with the
courtroom all night and
should have been ready for
the first intimation of a
verdict. The news came
when an afternoon papers
service is, as a rule,
dormant.


The Associated Press was
counting on the biggest
scoop of the year, and pos
sibly 1t succeeded in some
cities, but beating the morn
ing paper in Spokane was
such an easy matter that
The Press almost feels like
apologizing for mentioning
it.
